hyperedges.label: Terraform Approval Gate Flow (CLI and Web)
tf9 UI Redesign Spec Lineage (redesign -> parity -> pixel-port)

nodes.label: tf9 CLI and Web UI Tool
nodes.norm_label: tf9 cli and web ui tool

nodes.label: Two Interfaces, One Binary
nodes.rationale: The CLI and the web UI share one binary so config, run history, and reports behave identically in both interfaces; the React SPA is embedded via go:embed so distribution is a single artifact.

nodes.label: internal/server HTTP Mux and PID Management
nodes.norm_label: internal/server http mux and pid management

nodes.label: Pixel-Port Off Cloudscape
nodes.rationale: The UI was intentionally ported off AWS Cloudscape onto a hand-rolled design system matching the original prototype; importing @cloudscape-design/components is forbidden to keep the dependency removed.

nodes.label: Hash-Based Routing via Page Union Type
nodes.norm_label: hash-based routing via page union type

nodes.label: Target Execution Order (Sequential Promotion)
nodes.rationale: Targets run in YAML list order so changes promote dev to staging to prod; apply stops on first failure and --parallel is forbidden for apply/destroy to keep state mutations safe.

nodes.label: CWD Mode (Run Without Configured Repo)
nodes.rationale: collectDirs checks if SearchRoot itself is a valid terraform directory before scanning subdirectories so tf9 works from any terraform module directory without a configured repo.

nodes.label: CLI Approval Gate (Native Terraform Prompt)
nodes.rationale: runTerraform deliberately omits -auto-approve unless --force is passed so terraform's own 'Enter a value:' prompt is shown; CLI wires cmd.Stdin to os.Stdin while the web path intercepts the same prompt via InputCh and approvalMonitor over SSE.

nodes.label: Embedded Dist Rebuild Constraint
nodes.rationale: internal/web/dist must be rebuilt before go build when frontend changes, otherwise go build silently embeds the old dist; make build handles the ordering.

nodes.label: Web UI Approval Gate (Amber Bar)
nodes.rationale: Deny ends the run with a distinct amber 'denied' status instead of red 'failed' so a deliberate operator rejection is distinguishable from a terraform error.

nodes.label: AWS SSO STS Validation Per Run
nodes.norm_label: aws sso sts validation per run

nodes.label: EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS Audit Trail
nodes.rationale: Every edge carries an honesty class so users can distinguish facts explicit in source from model inference; uncertain edges are flagged AMBIGUOUS rather than omitted or invented.

nodes.label: Structural AST Extraction (Part A)
nodes.rationale: AST extraction is deterministic and free, so it handles code structure while LLM tokens are reserved for semantic edges AST cannot find; both run in parallel to save 5-15s.

nodes.label: Semantic Extraction via Parallel Subagents (Part B)
nodes.rationale: Subagents must be dispatched in a single message to run in parallel and must be general-purpose (not read-only Explore) because they write chunk JSON files to disk as the success signal.
nodes.norm_label: semantic extraction via parallel subagents (part b)

nodes.label: Fast Path: Query Existing Graph
nodes.source_location: What You Must Do When Invoked
nodes.rationale: If graphify-out/graph.json already exists and the request is a natural-language question, skip rebuild entirely and query directly - the graph is persistent across sessions.

nodes.label: Graphify Honesty Rules
nodes.rationale: Never invent edges, always show token cost, never hide raw cohesion scores - the graph's value depends on a trustworthy audit trail.

nodes.label: Discrete Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Models follow discrete rubrics better than continuous ranges; production data showed a bimodal collapse (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+) when given a range, so the spec mandates exact values 0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55.

nodes.label: Deterministic Node ID Format
nodes.rationale: Semantic IDs must match the AST extractor's parent-dir + filename-stem format; using just the filename or the full path creates orphan ghost-duplicate nodes, and chunk-number suffixes break determinism.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Vocabulary Expansion
nodes.rationale: The query CLI matches via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming or synonyms, so the question must be expanded against the graph's actual vocabulary - never invented tokens - or a wording mismatch collapses the answer to noise.

nodes.label: save-result Q&A Feedback Loop
nodes.rationale: Answers are saved back into the graph so the next --update extracts each Q&A as a node, improving future queries.

nodes.label: Incremental Update (--update / build_merge)
nodes.rationale: Only changed files are re-extracted to save tokens; build_merge reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction is preserved (#801), and old nodes for changed files are pruned to avoid same-named symbol collapse (#1178). Code-only changes skip LLM extraction entirely.

nodes.label: Whisper Video/Audio Transcription
nodes.rationale: A domain-hint initial prompt is composed from the corpus's god node labels by the host LLM itself - no separate API call - improving Whisper accuracy on domain vocabulary.

nodes.label: Watch Mode Auto-Rebuild (--watch)
nodes.rationale: Code-only changes rebuild via AST with no LLM; doc/paper/image changes only set a needs_update flag because they require LLM semantic re-extraction. Debounce waits for file activity to stop so parallel agent writes do not trigger one rebuild per file.

nodes.label: Token Reduction Benchmark
nodes.rationale: Run only when total_words > 5000; for small corpora the graph's value is structural clarity, not token compression.

nodes.label: GitHub Clone and Cross-Repo Graph Merge
nodes.rationale: Subfolders are extracted with the CLI directly (each gets its own graphify-out inside the scanned path) because the skill pipeline would clobber a single shared output dir; merged nodes carry a repo attribute for origin filtering.
nodes.norm_label: github clone and cross-repo graph merge

nodes.label: tf9 Terraform Operations UI (Design Handoff)
nodes.rationale: Desktop web app for running and monitoring Terraform across multiple repositories, environments, and AWS profiles. The handoff bundle is a high-fidelity HTML/CSS/JS prototype, intentionally NOT production code: it is a pixel-perfect visual reference plus behavioral spec to be recreated in the real codebase's framework (React).

nodes.label: Cloudscape-Style Design Tokens (cloudscape.css)
nodes.rationale: All component colors are CSS custom properties (light palette in :root, dark overrides in theme.css) so dark mode becomes a single html[data-theme] attribute swap. Tokens cover color, typography (Open Sans 14px), radii (16px cards, 8px inputs, 20px pills) and shadows.

nodes.label: Live Streaming Split Panel (bottom/side dock)
nodes.rationale: Two dock modes (bottom: min(440px,52vh); side: min(640px,48%)) with drag resize handles so users can watch streaming terraform output without leaving the runs table. Header carries run ID, status badge, Re-run actions and dock toggles; body is meta-strip, progress bar, target dots, then output.

nodes.label: Terminal Color Tokens
nodes.rationale: The terminal output area is the ONLY component with its own token set instead of the shared design tokens — it adapts both light (#f6f8fa card) and dark (#0b1220 card) modes with dedicated add/delete/change/plan/error line colors so terraform diff semantics stay readable in either theme.

nodes.label: New Run Modal
nodes.rationale: Two-column modal: scrollable form left, sticky CLI preview rail right (the assembled terraform command with syntax highlighting). Safety-driven behavior: apply/destroy force Promotion mode and hide the Parallel option; force-unlock reveals per-target Lock ID inputs producing --lock-ids dev:abc123,staging:def456. Disabled targets (tf9-repo-overrides) are filtered out entirely.

nodes.label: Promotion Execution Mode (sequential, stop on first failure)
nodes.rationale: Promotion runs targets sequentially in pipeline order and stops on the first failure; Parallel runs all targets simultaneously but is deliberately disabled for apply/destroy to prevent uncontrolled blast radius. Promotion-mode output renders as stacked collapsible sections, each target starting only after the previous completes.
nodes.source_location: Pages > 3. New Run Modal > Execution Mode
nodes.norm_label: promotion execution mode (sequential, stop on first failure)

nodes.label: Repository Pipeline Groups (swim-lane stages, drag reorder)
nodes.norm_label: repository pipeline groups (swim-lane stages, drag reorder)

nodes.label: tf9-repo-overrides localStorage Persistence
nodes.rationale: Per-target disabled state and pipeline-group override persist client-side under localStorage key tf9-repo-overrides as {"repo:target": {disabled, group}}. The New Run Modal reads this key on load and filters out disabled targets, hiding empty groups — keeping the prototype stateful without a backend.

nodes.label: STS Authentication Badge
nodes.rationale: Topnav pill replacing the region label with three states: checking (amber pulsing, ~900ms simulating an AWS STS GetCallerIdentity call), ok/Authenticated (green glow dot), fail/Unauthenticated (red). Prototype toggles on click and persists under localStorage key tf9-sts-auth; the real implementation calls STS GetCallerIdentity.

nodes.label: Dark/Light Theming via html[data-theme]
nodes.rationale: Theme is controlled by html[data-theme=dark|light], defaulting to prefers-color-scheme and persisted under localStorage key tf9-color-mode. theme.js runs in <head> before first paint to avoid FOUC. Everything theme-switches through CSS custom properties except the terminal area which has its own dual-mode tokens.

nodes.label: Plan/Apply/Destroy Report Pages (shared layout)
nodes.rationale: Three report pages share one layout: sticky run-metadata header, aggregated add/change/destroy summary strip, then per-environment collapsible terminal sections. Blocks with changes auto-expand while no-change blocks start collapsed so reviewers see signal first; each block offers copy and {command}-{env}.txt download.

nodes.label: Reports History Page (filter chips, cards/list views)
nodes.norm_label: reports history page (filter chips, cards/list views)

nodes.label: Config YAML Editor Concept
nodes.rationale: A real in-browser code editor for the generated ~/.config/tf9/config.yaml: gutter, syntax highlighting, current-line band, live schema validation with a problems pane — because CLI and web UI share the same config file and edits must be validated before save.

nodes.label: Config YAML Editor Page Prototype (Config YAML.html)
nodes.norm_label: config yaml editor page prototype (config yaml.html)

nodes.label: Help Page Prototype (Help.html): quick start, config schema, CLI commands, sensitive-data notes, TOC scroll-spy, copy-to-clipboard
nodes.norm_label: help page prototype (help.html): quick start, config schema, cli commands, sensitive-data notes, toc scroll-spy, copy-to-clipboard

nodes.label: Config 'disabled' Target Flag (documented in Help)
nodes.rationale: Optional per-target YAML field `disabled` that skips the target in promotion and parallel runs — the server-side/config counterpart for excluding targets from execution. Help also documents `account_id` as optional, verified against STS before runs.

nodes.label: sts.js — Shared STS Badge Component Script
nodes.norm_label: sts.js — shared sts badge component script

nodes.label: Runs Split Panel (prototype)
nodes.rationale: Dockable (bottom/side) resizable panel that streams a selected run's output; the dock-toggle and drag handle let operators keep the runs table visible while watching live terraform output.

nodes.label: Fullscreen Terminal Modal (prototype)
nodes.rationale: macOS traffic-light styled fullscreen terminal with Copy and Download actions so long terraform output can be inspected and exported without leaving the Runs page.

nodes.label: Pipeline Swim-lane View (prototype)
nodes.rationale: Promotion pipeline rendered as horizontal stage lanes with a Pipeline/Table toggle, edit modal, and a browse section to add any directory containing .tf files as a new stage; encodes that apply/destroy run targets top-to-bottom and stop on first failure.

nodes.label: CLI Preview Summary Rail (prototype)
nodes.rationale: Sticky right-hand rail showing the assembled terraform command with syntax highlighting so users see exactly what the web UI will execute, preserving CLI transparency in the GUI.

nodes.label: tf9 UI Prototype Parity Design Spec
nodes.author: brainstorming session
nodes.norm_label: tf9 ui prototype parity design spec

nodes.label: Cloudscape Approximation Approach
nodes.rationale: Keep the real @cloudscape-design/components library and approximate the prototype with Cloudscape tokens. Justification: the prototype JS is wired to fake data, so rewriting behavior against the real backend is ~80% of the effort regardless of visual foundation; building on existing React+Cloudscape reuses ~3,000 working backend-wired lines and gives accessibility for free. Pixel-identical rendering explicitly out of scope.

nodes.label: Verbatim Pixel-Port Approach
nodes.rationale: Drop @cloudscape-design/components entirely and copy the prototype's cloudscape.css + theme.css verbatim, rebuilding each page as plain JSX, because this is 'the only path to genuine pixel parity'. Second locked decision: keep all real backend integration (api.ts/types.ts data flow), with prototype mock data-*.js as reference only except the intentional localStorage contracts tf9-color-mode and tf9-repo-overrides. Supersedes the earlier Cloudscape-approximation approach.

nodes.label: Dual-format SSE Detection
nodes.rationale: Option A: frontend tries JSON.parse({env,line}) on each SSE message and falls back to plain-line parsing via parseEnvSections. Chosen so the UI can ship frontend-first against the current plain-line backend stream, and when the Go backend later emits structured JSON SSE the frontend picks it up automatically with no further changes.

nodes.label: STS Auth Badge Component
nodes.rationale: Topnav pill with checking/ok/fail states showing AWS SSO session validity; persists last known state to localStorage to avoid flashing, then re-checks after ~900ms. Pixel-port spec rewires it from localStorage simulation to the real awsApi identity call.

nodes.label: tf9-repo-overrides localStorage Contract (useRepoOverrides)
nodes.rationale: Per-target {disabled, group} overrides keyed by repo:target stored in localStorage key tf9-repo-overrides. Used because the redesign pass forbade changing the config.yaml schema or backend; the pixel-port spec keeps it as one of the two intentional localStorage contracts (alongside tf9-color-mode).

nodes.label: PipelineView Component Spec
nodes.rationale: Horizontal swim-lane group view of repo targets; effective group = localStorage override or first directory segment (a deliberate behavior change from the existing toGroupRows which used the last segment). Env dot colors by name heuristic: prod=red, staging=amber, global=purple, dev=green.

nodes.label: FullscreenTerminal Component Spec
nodes.rationale: Full-page terminal overlay with decorative macOS traffic lights, clipboard Copy, and Blob-based Download named {command}-{env}.txt; closes via Esc, backdrop click, or button.

nodes.label: GET /api/aws/identity STS Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Identified backend gap: no HTTP route exposed aws sts get-caller-identity, so the STS badge had nothing real to query. Spec adds internal/aws Identity(profile) running get-caller-identity and a GET /api/aws/identity route returning {account, arn, userId} or the standard {error:{code,message}} envelope.

nodes.label: FOUC-preventing Theme Init (tf9-color-mode)
nodes.rationale: Inline <head> script reads localStorage tf9-color-mode before first paint and sets html[data-theme] to avoid a flash of the wrong theme; default follows prefers-color-scheme. The same contract is embedded in the prototype report pages and in generated standalone HTML reports so they theme-match the SPA.
